{{Quote|Hello dad.|Boba Fett while looking at his father's face in the mirror|<ref name="Being Boba Fett">{{Tales|18|Being Boba Fett}}</ref>}}
"'''Being Boba Fett'''" is a twelve-page comic featured in ''[[Star Wars Tales 18]]'', published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[December 24]], [[2003]]. It was later collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 5]]'', release [[February 23]], [[2005]]. ''Being Boba Fett'' was written by [[Jason Hall]] with art provided by [[Stewart McKenny]], [[John Wycough]], and [[David Nestelle]]. The story depicts a day in the life of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].

==Plot summary==
The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] wakes up in his home, and after looking in the mirror and seeing his [[Jango Fett/Legends|father's]] face, Boba sets out to look for his daily [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]]. After passing up escaped [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] and high-ranking [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] defectors, he settles for a dead-or-alive bounty placed on a [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] named [[Bendu Fry]]. Fett goes into a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], where Fry is telling a joke to two women, and as Fry says the punch line, Fett approaches. Fry tries to run, but Fett easily catches the fat Devaronian. To save his life, Bendu tells Fett of a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who is living on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Fett leaves to track the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] down, and seems to locate his target. It turns out, however, that the Twi'lek is not the Jedi after all, but the Jedi's son. His father had in fact been killed at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], and when the son grew up, he had a son of his own. Fett, remembering how his own father had perished in the Battle of [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], allows the Jedi to live not wanting the boy to be left fatherless like he was. He returns to the cantina, disintegrates Bendu and collects his bounty.

==Development==
Spanning twelve pages, ''Being Boba Fett'' was featured in ''[[Star Wars Tales 18]]'', published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[December 24]], [[2003]]. It was collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 5]]'', which was in turn released on [[February 23]], [[2005]]. The story was written by [[Jason Hall]]; the penciller was [[Stewart McKenny]], with colors done by [[John Wycough]] and [[David Nestelle]] providing the inking. Hall's inspiration for the story involved the idea that Boba Fett would "have to wake up every morning and be reminded that his father was dead (which would really mess you up after a while, and certainly rile up any feelings of hatred toward the Jedi)."<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/14594988|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official Jason Hall Discussion Thread|user=[[Jason Hall|Nerf-Lover]]|date=2004-01-16|time=8:06 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150908121146/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-jason-hall-discussion-thread.7574759/page-5}}</ref> ''Being Boba Fett'' appeared in an issue that was specifically dedicated to stories about Boba Fett.

==Continuity==
Owing to the fact that ''Being Boba Fett'' was published in an issue of ''Star Wars Tales'' before [[Star Wars Tales 21|Issue 21]], the [[canon]]icity of this story was originally ambiguous.<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=752#14952461|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-06-04|time=3:05 AM|quote=Consider everything that's not completely outrageous or intentionally comic as S-canon. If it's referenced in another non-Tales source, then elevate it to C-canon.}}</ref> ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]], included details from the story in the entry for Bendu Fry, though, making ''Being Boba Fett'' fully canonical.
